markers.title: Icon-only top nav — functions undiscoverable
markers.body: Four icons (housekeeping, filters, calendar, add) sit beside the date with no labels. A front-desk user encountering this for the first time has no way to know what each does. Add labels or tooltips, or at minimum use universally recognised icons.

markers.title: Unallocated reservation toggle — unclear intent
markers.body: The toggle on the 'unallocated reservation' banner has no label. It's unclear whether toggling it shows/hides the row, auto-assigns the room, or triggers a different workflow. Label the toggle action explicitly (e.g. 'Show only' or 'Assign').

markers.title: Filter tabs unequal widths — visual imbalance
markers.body: 'Stay-throughs' is noticeably wider than 'Check-ins' and 'Check-outs', making the tab row feel unstructured. Use a segmented control with equal-width segments, or at minimum ensure consistent horizontal padding across all tabs.

markers.title: 'Check in' prioritised over blocked payment
markers.body: When 'Payment request expired' is shown, the green 'Check in' CTA still draws the eye as the primary action. A guest can be checked in before payment is resolved — intentional? If not, dim or remove 'Check in' until payment is resolved, and make 'Take payment' the primary button.

markers.title: Inconsistent card layout within same section
markers.body: Cards 1 and 2 (Vince Abbott, Xena Chase) show dual buttons; card 3 (Alice Foster) shows a single full-width 'Check in · 7 nights' button. The inconsistency is jarring in the same 'Check-ins' section — apply a consistent pattern across all cards.

working_well: Date + day-strip combo at top is clear — today (FRI 26) is highlighted with underline, past days visible but muted.
CONFIRMED status badge in green gives immediate booking state at a glance.
Date range + nights count ('26 Jun → 1 Jul · 5 nights') is a highly legible format — no ambiguity.
The 'Check-ins / Check-outs / Stay-throughs' filter trio is the right level of granularity for a front-desk workflow.
worth_considering: Surface guest photo or avatar only when real images are available — identical placeholder photos across all cards reduces rather than aids scannability.
Show a count badge on each filter tab (e.g. 'Check-ins (3)') so staff can quickly see workload without switching tabs.
Group the top-right icons into a clear overflow menu or labelled icon bar to reduce cognitive load.
